titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Robert B. Stevens Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Matthew T. MacLean

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid created in accordance with manuscripts and archives' processing manual.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday December 1, 2018 at 08:33

did.abstract: The papers consist of reports, correspondence, and working papers documenting the professional life of Robert Stevens and the activities of the E.A.C.S.O., the East African Community, and the Commission on East African Cooperation.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from the African Collection Curator, 1995.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Robert B. Stevens Papers (MS 1686). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in four series: I. East African Common Services Organization, 1961-1966. II. Commission on East African Cooperation, 1966. III. East African Community, 1966-1974. IV. Subject Files, 1963-1973.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains reports, correspondence, and working papers documenting the professional life of Robert Stevens and the activities of the E.A.C.S.O., the East African Community, and the Commission on East African Cooperation.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Robert Bocking Stevens was born in 1933. He received his legal education from Oxford University and Yale University, and then practiced law in New York and London. In 1959 he began teaching at the Yale University Law School, where he became a professor of law in 1964. He also taught law at Oxford, the London School of Economics, Northwestern, Stanford, the University of Texas, and the University of East Africa. Stevens specialized in commercial law, jurisprudence, and legal history. In the 1960s he served as a legal and constitutional advisor to the East African Common Services Organization (E.A.C.S.O.) And its successor, the East African Community. These organizations managed the East African Common Market and various joint economic services provided for member states. The founding members were Uganda, Kenya, and Tanganyika (later Tanzania). Stevens also advised the Commission on East African Cooperation (C.E.A.C.), which oversaw the transition between the E.A.C.S.O. and the East African Community.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains papers related to the operations and policies of the East African Common Services Organization, and commentaries and opinions on that organization. It is arranged alphabetically by subject.

did.unittitle: General report to Commission on East African Cooperation [on state of E.A.C.S.O.]
East African Common Market economic data
Trade report [international trade within East Africa]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains papers documenting the operations, policies, and recommendations of the Commission on East African Cooperation. The series is arranged alphabetically by subject, except for the working papers of the Commission, which are arranged as originally written.

did.unittitle: Commentary on East African Common Services Organization management
Constitutional and legal problems [of East African Common Services Organization]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains papers related to the East African Community, which superseded the East African Common Services Organization in 1967. It is arranged alphabetically by subject.

did.unittitle: Commentary on East African Common Market
Applications for admission [to East African Community]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series is arranged alphabetically by subject, and contains papers documenting the professional life of Robert B. Stevens.

did.unittitle: Julius K. Nyerere speech [President of Tanganyika]
